results.abstract: This paper constructs examples of closed 10-manifolds with uniformly positive Ricci curvature whose fundamental groups cannot be uniformly virtually abelian, addressing questions left open by work of Kapovitch and Wilking.
results.title: Compact Manifolds with Unbounded Nilpotent Fundamental Groups and Positive Ricci Curvature

results.abstract: The paper proves that compact manifolds of dimension at least 12 with positive isotropic curvature are diffeomorphic to a spherical space form, a quotient of the round cylinder, or a connected sum of such manifolds.
results.title: Classification of Compact Manifolds with Positive Isotropic Curvature

results.abstract: This work constructs compact manifolds and algebraic varieties with extremely large symmetry groups, including diffeomorphism groups containing every finite nilpotent group of bounded class and rank.
results.title: Constructing Highly Symmetric Compact Manifolds and Algebraic Varieties

results.abstract: The authors study r-fundamental groups of directed graphs and connect them to the magnitude-path spectral sequence through Hurewicz and Seifert-van Kampen type results.
results.title: The Fundamental Group and the Magnitude-Path Spectral Sequence of a Directed Graph

results.abstract: This paper defines a Novikov fundamental group associated to a cohomology class on a closed manifold and uses it to derive lower bounds for critical points of Morse closed 1-forms.
results.title: A Novikov Fundamental Group

results.abstract: The paper develops the basic theory of the fundamental groupoid in discrete homotopy theory, including covering graphs and a discrete Seifert-van Kampen theorem.
results.title: The Fundamental Group in Discrete Homotopy Theory

results.abstract: This work gives a presentation of the GL_n(C)-equivariant cohomology ring with integer coefficients of the representation variety Hom(Z^2, GL_n(C)).
results.title: The Equivariant Cohomology Ring of the Representation Variety Hom(Z^2, GL_n(C))

results.abstract: The paper generalizes a result of Cohen and Suciu by describing the cohomology ring of the boundary manifold of an arbitrary combinatorial line arrangement, including non-realizable ones.
results.title: The Cohomology Ring of the Boundary Manifold of a Combinatorial Line Arrangement

results.abstract: This paper describes the cohomology ring of toric wonderful models for arbitrary building sets using blowups of posets, Grobner bases over rings, and admissible functions.
results.title: Cohomology Rings of Toric Wonderful Model
